title: Myrteola phylicoides
taxonomy: Myrteola phylicoides is classified within the family Myrtaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the myrtle family. This family is part of the order Myrtales and the clade Rosids. Myrteola phylicoides is native to New Zealand and is a member of the genus Myrteola, which consists of two species. The genus Myrteola is closely related to the genus Lophomyrtus. The classification of Myrteola phylicoides is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic analysis.
uses: Myrteola phylicoides has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. In South African traditional medicine, it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and wounds.
